clinical_history: 66-yr-old male: what evidence (subtle) is there that he has chronic renal failure?
diagnosis: The tracing shows sinus rhythm with a prolonged PR interval, intraventricular conduction defect (IVCD), left atrial abnormality (LAA), probably underlying left ventricular hypertrophy (LVH) and probably underlying anteroseptal  myocardial infarction (MI).  Of note is the subtle T wave peaking in V5, with a slightly long QT (450 ms).  The former is consistent with hyperkalemia (5.9 mEq/L); the latter with possible hypocalcemia (level not available).  Sometimes hyperkalemia produces only subtle T wave peaking, without absolutely tall T waves, as is the case here.
